mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of co-occurring periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of cases during early-cycle phases, or the autocorrelation of INDPRO dominates the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 70% of periods where monthly CPI exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 60% of observed quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows, or GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
